meaning: adj.(=illusory)幻影的,错觉的,迷惑人的,虚假的,不牢靠的
examples: Inspired by the famous 2d drawing of the optical illusive bookshelf.
But the killer applications for personal robots remain as illusive as ever.
The reason that happiness can seem illusive is because it is a bi-product of serving others.
Mawston does not believe that Apple will launch the illusive iPhone Mini this year.
Rather, it was misinformed journalists who talked of an island of plastic, mythological masses of synthetic detritus, an illusive terra aqua.
